description: Analyze office-to-residential conversion viability in San Francisco using the notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/office_conversion.ipynb and data from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5. Identify candidate office buildings (building_type_id 4, built between 1801 and 1989, >= 50,000 non-residential sqft, 0 residential units, and valid sales price) and calculate a viability score using a weighted average of normalized building age (40%, where age = 2026 - year_built) and inverse normalized value per sqft (60%, where value_per_sqft = residential_sales_price / non_residential_sqft). Potential units are defined as the floor of non-residential square footage divided by 1000. Export the top 50 candidates by viability score to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/top_conversion_candidates.csv, including building_id, parcel_id, zone_id, year_built, value_per_sqft, potential_new_units, and viability_score. Aggregate total potential units and candidate counts by zone_id and export to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_conversion_capacity.csv, sorted descending by total units. Additionally, generate a scatter plot of building age vs value per sqft colored by viability score and save it to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/conversion_scatter.png.
